mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 340i or M340i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned B58 engine typically costs £3,200–£7,500 supply only depending on which generation (F30 or G20), with used units available from £1,500–£3,500. Fitting adds £1,800–£2,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 340i / M340i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. It's a significant improvement over the N55 and shares its architecture with the bulletproof B58 used across BMW's range and in the Toyota Supra. The main weaknesses are the cooling system, oil filter housing, and PCV valve — all are well-understood and repairable.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 340i and M340i?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cooling system leaks and water pump failure, oil filter housing cracking (subject to a class action lawsuit), and PCV valve failure causing misfires and rough idle. The B58 is fundamentally robust — these are maintenance items, not catastrophic design flaws.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 340i / M340i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: 340i and M340i values remain strong — a well-kept G20 M340i is often worth £25,000–£45,000+. A £3,200–£7,500 reconditioned engine is only 10–25% of the vehicle's value — well within the 70% BMW threshold. Replacement is almost always worthwhile.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 340i or M340i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. F30 340i (2015–2019) uses the B58B30M0; G20 M340i (2019–present) uses the B58B30O1. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the B58 cooling system, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cooling system components need replacement — they are wear items. The water pump (£500–£1,200) and plastic coolant hoses should be replaced preventatively at around 60,000–80,000 miles. Upgraded aluminium parts are available to replace the factory plastic components. Regular coolant level checks are essential — the B58 can lose coolant suddenly.

hero.h1: BMW 340i Engine Replacement — Compare Prices & Get Quotes
hero.subHeadline: The 340i and M340i are powered by BMW's legendary B58 straight-six — one of the most reliable modern turbo engines. The F30-generation B58B30M0 delivers 322–335 hp; the G20 M340i's B58B30O1 produces 382 hp. Get quotes from UK specialists for reconditioned, rebuilt and used units today.

problems.title: Cooling System Leaks and Water Pump Failure
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, rising temperature gauge, warning light, steam from the engine bay, heater not working properly, limp mode activation
problems.recommendation: The B58's cooling system is its most common weak point. The water pump can fail prematurely — one owner reported the water pump pulley disintegrating during hard acceleration. Coolant loss is frequently reported, with the primary reservoir dropping suddenly. Plastic coolant components (hoses, connectors, thermostat housing) become brittle with age. Upgraded aluminium coolant pipes are available to replace the factory plastic parts. Regular inspection of coolant levels and the water pump is essential — catch it before overheating.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant leak guide

problems.title: Oil Filter Housing Leaks
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the car, burning oil smell from the engine bay, low oil pressure warning, coolant loss, engine warning light
problems.repairCost: £800–£1,800 (oil filter housing replacement, including intake manifold removal)
problems.recommendation: The B58's oil filter housing is a known weak point. BMW faces a class action lawsuit over faulty oil filter housings that leak coolant, costing owners thousands in repairs. The housing can crack, causing coolant or oil leaks. Because it sits under the intake manifold, replacement is labour-intensive. Oil leaks from the valve cover gasket are also common. Check for oil spots regularly and investigate any drop in fluid levels promptly. Use high-quality oil filters — poor quality filters can disintegrate and cause housing wear.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil filter housing failure guide

problems.title: PCV Valve Failure
problems.symptoms: Misfires, rough idle, oil in the intake/chargepipe, oil leaks, crankcase pressure issues, check engine light
problems.repairCost: £400–£800 (PCV valve or pressure control valve replacement)
problems.recommendation: One of the most widely discussed B58 problems revolves around faulty crankcase pressure control valves, affecting many BMW models between 2017 and 2021. The PCV (positive crankcase ventilation) valve can fail, causing oil to be drawn into the intake, leading to misfires and rough idle. This issue is especially common on cars with upgraded turbos. Repair kits are available to address the problem without replacing the entire valve cover. Monitor oil consumption and listen for rough idle — if the PCV fails, the engine can run poorly and oil consumption increases.

failureLink.label: Read the full PCV valve failure guide

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 340i / M340i? Check These First
buyIf: * G20 M340i (2019+) — the B58TU brings meaningful improvements over the original F30 version
* Documented cooling system maintenance — water pump or hoses replaced at around 60,000–80,000 miles
* Full service history with correct BMW-spec oil — the B58 is reliable but unforgiving of neglect
* Evidence of oil filter housing inspection or replacement
avoidIf: * Evidence of repeated coolant top-ups without a diagnosed leak — the cooling system is the B58's Achilles' heel
* Oil spots under the car without a clear diagnosis — likely oil filter housing or valve cover leaks
* Poor service history — the B58 is robust but sensitive to oil quality
* Any engine warning light without a clear diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The B58 is one of BMW's finest modern engines — a genuine step forward in reliability from the N55 that preceded it. It's smooth, torquey, and durable. Its weaknesses are well-understood: the cooling system, oil filter housing, and PCV valve. Buy on history, maintain on schedule, and the B58 will reward you with exceptional performance and longevity. The G20 M340i's B58TU represents the most refined version of this engine.

cta.label: → Compare 340i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 340i or M340i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Coolant Level — Any unexplained loss? (cooling system warning — check the primary reservoir)
2. Service History — including which generation (F30 or G20) and evidence of cooling system maintenance
3. Oil Spots — Any oil under the car? (oil filter housing or valve cover leak warning)
4. Cold Start — Any rattles? (timing chain issues are rare on B58 but possible)
5. Idle Quality — Any rough idle or misfires? (PCV or injector issues)
6. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
7. Turbo Performance — Test acceleration; any hesitation, whistling, or wastegate rattle?
8. MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (catalyst, O2 sensors)

items.question: Section 9: The trust and final CTA section. Since the priority was Medium, I need to keep the "Assertive" mode consistent with the CTA from Part 1.
Meta Data: A title under 60 chars and a description under 160 chars, incorporating the key selling points and the fact it's a B58 engine.
Open Graph/Twitter Cards: Mirror the meta data.

JSON-LD Schema: This is critical. I must ensure the ItemList for the engine era map and the engine codes match the Era Map exactly (B58B30M0, B58B30O1). The Service schema's price range needs to span the full history (from £1,500 to £7,500). I also need to include all the required schema blocks: BreadcrumbList, FAQPage, Organization, Service, Dataset, WebPage, and explicitly omit AggregateRating (as per the rules, since we don't have genuine review data).

Production Note: This is where I show the thinking behind the schema choices and confirm the authority model compliance. I need to state the ratings are newly assigned, list the verification sources (from the Data Note in Part 1), and confirm schema consistency.
items.answer: I should structure the response cleanly, starting with "Part 2" as a header, then Section 9, then Meta, OG, Schema, and finally the Production Note. The tone should be professional and precise, mirroring the assistant's previous outputs. The user probably expects this to be copy-paste ready, so ensuring no markdown errors and proper formatting is key.
